properties.name: actualHeight
properties.doc: The actual height for this [ComponentView](tools.aqua.bgw.components/ComponentView) considering scale.

properties.name: actualPosX
properties.doc: Absolute horizontal position of this [ComponentView](tools.aqua.bgw.components/ComponentView) in the [Scene](tools.aqua.bgw.core/Scene). Does not take panning and zooming of a [CameraPane](tools.aqua.bgw.components.layoutviews/CameraPane) into account.

properties.name: actualPosY
properties.doc: Absolute vertical position of this [ComponentView](tools.aqua.bgw.components/ComponentView) in the [Scene](tools.aqua.bgw.core/Scene). Does not take panning and zooming of a [CameraPane](tools.aqua.bgw.components.layoutviews/CameraPane) into account.

properties.name: actualWidth
properties.doc: The actual width for this [ComponentView](tools.aqua.bgw.components/ComponentView) considering scale.

properties.name: backgroundStyle
properties.doc: Css style that gets applied to this [UIComponent](tools.aqua.bgw.components.uicomponents/UIComponent)'s background.

properties.name: componentStyle
properties.doc: Css style that gets applied to this [UIComponent](tools.aqua.bgw.components.uicomponents/UIComponent).

properties.name: dropAcceptor
properties.doc: Returns whether this [ComponentView](tools.aqua.bgw.components/ComponentView) is a valid drop target for the dragged component in the given [DragEvent](tools.aqua.bgw.event/DragEvent) or not.

properties.name: font
properties.doc: [Font](tools.aqua.bgw.util/Font) of this [UIComponent](tools.aqua.bgw.components.uicomponents/UIComponent). Usage depends on subclass.

properties.name: height
properties.doc: The [height](tools.aqua.bgw.components/ComponentView/height) for this [ComponentView](tools.aqua.bgw.components/ComponentView).

properties.name: inParentPosX
properties.doc: Relative horizontal position of this [ComponentView](tools.aqua.bgw.components/ComponentView) in its parent. Is equal to posX if the parent is not self-layouting like [CardStack](tools.aqua.bgw.components.container/CardStack), [HexagonGrid](tools.aqua.bgw.components.container/HexagonGrid), [LinearLayout](tools.aqua.bgw.components.container/LinearLayout) or [GridPane](tools.aqua.bgw.components.layoutviews/GridPane).

properties.name: inParentPosY
properties.doc: Relative vertical position of this [ComponentView](tools.aqua.bgw.components/ComponentView) in its parent. Is equal to posY if the parent is self-layouting like [CardStack](tools.aqua.bgw.components.container/CardStack), [HexagonGrid](tools.aqua.bgw.components.container/HexagonGrid), [LinearLayout](tools.aqua.bgw.components.container/LinearLayout) or [GridPane](tools.aqua.bgw.components.layoutviews/GridPane).

properties.name: isDisabled
properties.doc: Controls if user input events cause input functions of this [ComponentView](tools.aqua.bgw.components/ComponentView) to get invoked.

properties.name: isFocusable
properties.doc: Controls whether this [ComponentView](tools.aqua.bgw.components/ComponentView) is focusable or not.

properties.name: isLayoutFromCenter
properties.doc: Field that indicates whether posX and posY denote the center or top left of this [ComponentView](tools.aqua.bgw.components/ComponentView). Only relevant for GridPanes. Cannot be set manually.

properties.name: isVisible
properties.doc: Visibility of this [ComponentView](tools.aqua.bgw.components/ComponentView).

properties.name: layoutBounds
properties.doc: Returns a [CoordinatePlain](tools.aqua.bgw.util/CoordinatePlain) containing the component's corner Coordinates and its layout bounds.

properties.name: name
properties.doc: Name field only for debugging purposes. Has no effect on rendering.

properties.name: onDragDropped
properties.doc: Gets invoked with a [DragEvent](tools.aqua.bgw.event/DragEvent) whenever a drag and drop gesture finishes over this [ComponentView](tools.aqua.bgw.components/ComponentView) and the [dropAcceptor](tools.aqua.bgw.components/ComponentView/dropAcceptor) returns true for the given [DragEvent](tools.aqua.bgw.event/DragEvent).

properties.name: onDragGestureEntered
properties.doc: Gets invoked with a [DragEvent](tools.aqua.bgw.event/DragEvent) whenever the mouse enters this [ComponentView](tools.aqua.bgw.components/ComponentView) while performing a drag gesture.

properties.name: onDragGestureExited
properties.doc: Gets invoked with a [DragEvent](tools.aqua.bgw.event/DragEvent) whenever the mouse leaves this [ComponentView](tools.aqua.bgw.components/ComponentView) while performing a drag gesture.

properties.name: onKeyPressed
properties.doc: Gets invoked with a [KeyEvent](tools.aqua.bgw.event/KeyEvent) whenever a key is pressed while this [ComponentView](tools.aqua.bgw.components/ComponentView) has focus.

properties.name: onKeyReleased
properties.doc: Gets invoked with a [KeyEvent](tools.aqua.bgw.event/KeyEvent) whenever a key is released while this [ComponentView](tools.aqua.bgw.components/ComponentView) has focus.

properties.name: onKeyTyped
properties.doc: Gets invoked with a [KeyEvent](tools.aqua.bgw.event/KeyEvent) whenever a Character is typed while this [ComponentView](tools.aqua.bgw.components/ComponentView) has focus. Gets invoked after [onKeyPressed](tools.aqua.bgw.components/ComponentView/onKeyPressed).

properties.name: onMouseClicked
properties.doc: Gets invoked with a [MouseEvent](tools.aqua.bgw.event/MouseEvent) whenever the mouse is clicked inside this [ComponentView](tools.aqua.bgw.components/ComponentView). Gets invoked after [onMousePressed](tools.aqua.bgw.components/ComponentView/onMousePressed) and [onMouseReleased](tools.aqua.bgw.components/ComponentView/onMouseReleased).

properties.name: onMouseEntered
properties.doc: Gets invoked with a [MouseEvent](tools.aqua.bgw.event/MouseEvent) whenever the mouse enters this [ComponentView](tools.aqua.bgw.components/ComponentView).

properties.name: onMouseExited
properties.doc: Gets invoked with a [MouseEvent](tools.aqua.bgw.event/MouseEvent) whenever the mouse leaves this [ComponentView](tools.aqua.bgw.components/ComponentView).

properties.name: onMousePressed
properties.doc: Gets invoked with a [MouseEvent](tools.aqua.bgw.event/MouseEvent) whenever the mouse is pressed inside this [ComponentView](tools.aqua.bgw.components/ComponentView).

properties.name: onMouseReleased
properties.doc: Gets invoked with a [MouseEvent](tools.aqua.bgw.event/MouseEvent) whenever the mouse is released inside this [ComponentView](tools.aqua.bgw.components/ComponentView).

properties.name: onMouseWheel
properties.doc: Gets invoked with a [WheelEvent](tools.aqua.bgw.event/WheelEvent) whenever the mouse wheel is turned while the mouse is inside this [ComponentView](tools.aqua.bgw.components/ComponentView).

properties.name: opacity
properties.doc: Opacity of this [ComponentView](tools.aqua.bgw.components/ComponentView).

types.name: Button
types.doc: A simple [Button](tools.aqua.bgw.components.uicomponents/Button) with a text.

types.name: CheckBox
types.doc: A simple [CheckBox](tools.aqua.bgw.components.uicomponents/CheckBox) with a text.

types.name: ColorPicker
types.doc: A [ColorPicker](tools.aqua.bgw.components.uicomponents/ColorPicker) that allows to choose a [Color](tools.aqua.bgw.core/Color).

types.name: ComboBox
types.doc: A standard [ComboBox](tools.aqua.bgw.components.uicomponents/ComboBox) that may be populated with items of specified type parameter.

types.name: Label
types.doc: A basic [Label](tools.aqua.bgw.components.uicomponents/Label) displaying text.

types.name: LabeledUIComponent
types.doc: Baseclass for all UIComponents that have a label.

types.name: ListView
types.doc: A [ListView](tools.aqua.bgw.components.uicomponents/ListView) displaying its items next to each other in the given orientation.

types.name: Orientation
types.doc: [Enum](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-enum/index.html) for orientations HORIZONTAL and VERTICAL.

types.name: PasswordField
types.doc: A [PasswordField](tools.aqua.bgw.components.uicomponents/PasswordField) is a single line input field that shows stars instead of typed text.

types.name: ProgressBar
types.doc: A [ProgressBar](tools.aqua.bgw.components.uicomponents/ProgressBar).

types.name: RadioButton
types.doc: [RadioButton](tools.aqua.bgw.components.uicomponents/RadioButton) is analogous to a ToggleButton with a different visual representation but cannot be deselected.

types.name: SelectionMode
types.doc: [Enum](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-enum/index.html) indicating allowed selection mode.

types.name: StructuredDataView
types.doc: A [StructuredDataView](tools.aqua.bgw.components.uicomponents/StructuredDataView) displaying its items with given format function.

types.name: TableColumn
types.doc: A [TableColumn](tools.aqua.bgw.components.uicomponents/TableColumn) may be used to represent a column in a TableView.

types.name: TableView
types.doc: A [TableView](tools.aqua.bgw.components.uicomponents/TableView) may be used to visualize a data table.

types.name: TextArea
types.doc: A [TextArea](tools.aqua.bgw.components.uicomponents/TextArea) is a multi line input field.

types.name: TextField
types.doc: A [TextField](tools.aqua.bgw.components.uicomponents/TextField) is a single line input field.

types.name: TextInputUIComponent
types.doc: Baseclass for all UIComponents that have a text input field.

types.name: ToggleButton
types.doc: A [ToggleButton](tools.aqua.bgw.components.uicomponents/ToggleButton) may be used as a Button that is either selected or not selected. An important feature of ToggleButtons is the [ToggleGroup](tools.aqua.bgw.components.uicomponents/ToggleGroup).

types.name: ToggleGroup
types.doc: A [ToggleGroup](tools.aqua.bgw.components.uicomponents/ToggleGroup) may be set as an attribute in ToggleButton or RadioButton.

info.name: BoardGameApplication
info.signature: constructor(windowTitle: String = DEFAULT_WINDOW_TITLE, width: Number = DEFAULT_WINDOW_WIDTH, height: Number = DEFAULT_WINDOW_HEIGHT, windowMode: WindowMode? = null)
info.doc: Creates the BoardGameApplication with optional title and given width / height. May only be called once per execution.

parameters.name: windowTitle
parameters.doc: Title for the application window. Gets displayed in the title bar. Default: [DEFAULT_WINDOW_TITLE](DEFAULT_WINDOW_TITLE).

parameters.name: windowMode
parameters.doc: Initial window mode. Overrides [isMaximized](tools.aqua.bgw.core/isMaximized) and [isFullScreen](tools.aqua.bgw.core/isFullScreen) if passed. Refer to [WindowMode](WindowMode) docs for further information about the effects.

functions.name: loadFont
functions.doc: Loads a font file and registers it in the GUI. The default font weight is [Font.FontWeight.NORMAL](tools.aqua.bgw.util/Font/FontWeight/NORMAL). The font name is derived from the file name without the file extension.

functions.signature: fun loadFont(path: String, fontName: String, weight: Font.FontWeight): Boolean
functions.name: loadFont
functions.doc: Loads a font file and registers it in the GUI.

functions.name: runOnGUIThread
functions.doc: Executes given [task](tools.aqua.bgw.core/BoardGameApplication/Companion/runOnGUIThread) on the UI thread. Use this method to update properties of ComponentViews from asynchronous environments like network callbacks. If no Application has yet been started, the [task](tools.aqua.bgw.core/BoardGameApplication/Companion/runOnGUIThread) is executed on the calling Thread. This function is Thread safe.

info.name: background
info.doc:
Background [Visual](tools.aqua.bgw.visual/Visual) for the [BoardGameApplication](tools.aqua.bgw.core/BoardGameApplication).

It is visible in the space that appears if the application window ratio does not fit the [Scene](tools.aqua.bgw.core/Scene) ratio.

Do not mix up with the [Scene.background](tools.aqua.bgw.visual/Visual)ua.bgw.core/Scene).background.

info.name: exit
info.doc: Returns the [show](tools.aqua.bgw.core/BoardGameApplication/show) function, thus closing the application window.

info.name: fullscreenExitCombinationHint
info.doc:
Specifies the KeyCombination hint that will be shown upon entering fullscreen mode. Hint will only be shown if an exit combination other than 'null' has been set.

A value of 'null' will result in the default text being shown. An empty string will result in no text being shown despite [fullscreenExitCombination](tools.aqua.bgw.core/BoardGameApplication/fullscreenExitCombination) being set.

info.name: fullscreenExitCombination
info.doc:
Specifies the KeyCombination that will allow the user to exit full screen mode. A value of 'null' will not match any [KeyEvent](tools.aqua.bgw.event/KeyEvent) and will make it so the user is not able to escape from fullscreen mode.

The 'character' field in the [KeyEvent](tools.aqua.bgw.event/KeyEvent) is unused.

info.name: hideMenuScene
info.doc: Hides currently shown [MenuScene](tools.aqua.bgw.core/MenuScene). Activates [BoardGameScene](tools.aqua.bgw.core/BoardGameScene) if present.

parameters.name: fadeTime
parameters.doc: Time to fade out in milliseconds. Default: [DEFAULT_FADE_TIME](tools.aqua.bgw.core/DEFAULT_FADE_TIME).

info.name: icon
info.doc: Window icon displayed in the title and task bar.

info.name: BoardGameApplication
info.signature: open class BoardGameApplication(windowTitle: String = DEFAULT_WINDOW_TITLE, width: Number = DEFAULT_WINDOW_WIDTH, height: Number = DEFAULT_WINDOW_HEIGHT, windowMode: WindowMode? = null)
info.doc:
Baseclass for all BGW Applications. Extend from this class in order to create your own game application. You may only instantiate one application.

Scenes get shown by calling [showMenuScene](tools.aqua.bgw.core/BoardGameApplication/showMenuScene) and [showGameScene](tools.aqua.bgw.core/BoardGameApplication/showGameScene). Application starts by calling [show](tools.aqua.bgw.core/BoardGameApplication/show).

parameters.name: windowTitle
parameters.doc: Title for the application window. Gets displayed in the title bar. Default: [DEFAULT_WINDOW_TITLE](tools.aqua.bgw.core/DEFAULT_WINDOW_TITLE).

parameters.name: windowMode
parameters.doc: Initial window mode. Overrides [isMaximized](tools.aqua.bgw.core/BoardGameApplication/isMaximized) and [isFullScreen](tools.aqua.bgw.core/BoardGameApplication/isFullScreen) if passed. Refer to [WindowMode](tools.aqua.bgw.core/WindowMode) docs for further information about the effects.

throws.name: IllegalStateException
throws.doc: If a second application is created.

constructors.signature: constructor(windowTitle: String = DEFAULT_WINDOW_TITLE, width: Number = DEFAULT_WINDOW_WIDTH, height: Number = DEFAULT_WINDOW_HEIGHT, windowMode: WindowMode? = null)

parameters.name: windowTitle
parameters.doc: Title for the application window. Gets displayed in the title bar. Default: [DEFAULT_WINDOW_TITLE](tools.aqua.bgw.core/DEFAULT_WINDOW_TITLE).

parameters.name: windowMode
parameters.doc: Initial window mode. Overrides [isMaximized](tools.aqua.bgw.core/BoardGameApplication/isMaximized) and [isFullScreen](tools.aqua.bgw.core/BoardGameApplication/isFullScreen) if passed. Refer to [WindowMode](tools.aqua.bgw.core/WindowMode) docs for further information about the effects.

constructors.doc: Creates the BoardGameApplication with optional title and given width / height. May only be called once per execution.

properties.name: background
properties.doc: Background [Visual](tools.aqua.bgw.visual/Visual) for the [BoardGameApplication](tools.aqua.bgw.core/BoardGameApplication).

properties.name: fullscreenExitCombination
properties.doc: Specifies the KeyCombination that will allow the user to exit full screen mode. A value of 'null' will not match any KeyEvent and will make it so the user is not able to escape from fullscreen mode.

properties.name: fullscreenExitCombinationHint
properties.doc: Specifies the KeyCombination hint that will be shown upon entering fullscreen mode. Hint will only be shown if an exit combination other than 'null' has been set.

properties.name: icon
properties.doc: Window icon displayed in the title and task bar.

properties.name: isFullScreen
properties.doc: Sets this [BoardGameApplication](tools.aqua.bgw.core/BoardGameApplication)'s fullscreen mode. true for fullscreen mode, false for default window.

properties.name: isMaximized
properties.doc: Sets this [BoardGameApplication](tools.aqua.bgw.core/BoardGameApplication)'s maximized mode.

properties.name: onWindowClosed
properties.doc: Gets invoked after the application window was closed.

properties.name: onWindowShown
properties.doc: Gets invoked when the application was started and the window was shown.

properties.name: title

info.signature: open class BoardGameScene(width: Number = DEFAULT_SCENE_WIDTH, height: Number = DEFAULT_SCENE_HEIGHT, background: Visual = ColorVisual.WHITE) : Scene<ComponentView>
info.doc:
Baseclass for BGW game scenes.

Extend this class in order to create your own game scene.

properties.name: background
properties.doc: The background [Visual](tools.aqua.bgw.visual/Visual) of this [Scene](tools.aqua.bgw.core/Scene).

properties.name: backgroundOpacity
properties.doc: Opacity of the background of this [Scene](tools.aqua.bgw.core/Scene).

properties.name: components
properties.doc: Returns all root components that are currently contained in this [Scene](tools.aqua.bgw.core/Scene).

properties.name: height
properties.doc: The height of this [Scene](tools.aqua.bgw.core/Scene) in virtual coordinates.

properties.name: onKeyPressed
properties.doc: Gets invoked with a [KeyEvent](tools.aqua.bgw.event/KeyEvent) whenever a key is pressed.

properties.name: onKeyReleased
properties.doc: Gets invoked with a [KeyEvent](tools.aqua.bgw.event/KeyEvent) whenever a key is released.

properties.name: onKeyTyped
properties.doc: Gets invoked with a [KeyEvent](tools.aqua.bgw.event/KeyEvent) whenever a Character is typed. Gets invoked after [onKeyPressed](tools.aqua.bgw.core/Scene/onKeyPressed) .

properties.name: onLockChanged
properties.doc: Gets invoked whenever the [lock](tools.aqua.bgw.core/BoardGameScene/lock) state of this [BoardGameScene](tools.aqua.bgw.core/BoardGameScene) changes.

properties.name: onSceneHidden
properties.doc: Gets invoked with no event whenever a scene gets hidden.

properties.name: onSceneRescaled
properties.doc: Gets invoked whenever the scene is rescaled because the window was resized.

properties.name: onSceneShown
properties.doc: Gets invoked with no event whenever a scene gets shown.

properties.name: opacity
properties.doc: Opacity of the background of this [Scene](tools.aqua.bgw.core/Scene).

properties.name: rootNode
properties.doc: The root node of this [Scene](tools.aqua.bgw.core/Scene).

properties.name: width
properties.doc: The width of this [Scene](tools.aqua.bgw.core/Scene) in virtual coordinates.

functions.name: addComponents
functions.doc: Adds all given ComponentViews to the root node and rootComponents list.

functions.name: clearComponents
functions.doc: Removes all ComponentViews from the root node and rootComponents list.

functions.name: findPathToChild
functions.doc: Searches [node](tools.aqua.bgw.core/Scene/findPathToChild) recursively through the visual tree and logs path where the [node](tools.aqua.bgw.core/Scene/findPathToChild) appears as first component and the [rootNode](tools.aqua.bgw.core/Scene/rootNode) as last.

functions.name: lock
functions.doc: Locks [Scene](tools.aqua.bgw.core/Scene) from any user input.

functions.name: playAnimation
functions.doc: Plays given [Animation](tools.aqua.bgw.animation/Animation) non-blocking. This means that any subsequent updates may be executed before the [Animation](tools.aqua.bgw.animation/Animation) actually starts playing.

functions.name: removeComponents
functions.doc: Removes all given ComponentViews from the root node and rootComponents list.

functions.name: stopAllAnimations
functions.doc: Stops all currently playing Animations. This will NOT revert persisted animations that already finished. It will however cancel so that onFinished callbacks do not get invoked.

functions.name: unlock
functions.doc: Unlocks [Scene](tools.aqua.bgw.core/Scene) for user input.

info.name: FULLSCREEN
info.doc:
Starts Application as maximized window. Overrides BoardGameApplication.isFullScreen as 'true'.

Note: This does not override BoardGameApplication.isMaximized which might become relevant after leaving fullscreen mode.

info.name: MAXIMIZED
info.doc: Starts Application as maximized non-fullscreen window. Overrides BoardGameApplication.isMaximized as 'true'. Overrides BoardGameApplication.isFullScreen as 'false'.

info.name: NORMAL
info.doc: Starts Application as normal window with given dimensions. Overrides BoardGameApplication.isMaximized as 'false'. Overrides BoardGameApplication.isFullScreen as 'false'.

Creates a Dialog.

For exception dialogs refer to exception constructor.

parameters.name: dialogType
parameters.doc: The [DialogType](DialogType) of the dialog. Affects the displayed icon.

parameters.name: header
parameters.doc: Headline to be shown in the dialogs content.

parameters.name: message
parameters.doc: Message to be shown in the dialogs content.

parameters.name: buttons
parameters.doc: Buttons to be shown. Standard set of buttons according to dialogType will be used if you don't pass any ButtonTypes.

info.name: Dialog
info.doc: Shows a dialog containing the given message and buttons.

properties.name: dialogType
properties.doc: The [DialogType](tools.aqua.bgw.dialog/DialogType) of the dialog. Affects the displayed icon.

properties.name: exception
properties.doc: [Throwable](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-throwable/index.html) to be shown in expandable content.

properties.name: header
properties.doc: Headline to be shown in the dialogs content.

properties.name: message
properties.doc: Message to be shown.

properties.name: onButtonClicked
properties.doc: Gets invoked whenever a button in the dialog is clicked.

properties.name: title
properties.doc: Title to be shown.

info.name: onButtonClicked
info.doc: Gets invoked whenever a button in the dialog is clicked.

info.name: CONFIRMATION
info.doc: The [CONFIRMATION](tools.aqua.bgw.dialog/DialogType/CONFIRMATION) dialog type configures the dialog to appear in a way that suggests the content of the dialog is seeking confirmation from the user. This includes a 'confirmation' image, an appropriate title and header, and both ButtonType.YES and ButtonType.NO buttons for the user to click on to dismiss the dialog.

info.name: ERROR
info.doc: The [ERROR](tools.aqua.bgw.dialog/DialogType/ERROR) dialog type configures the dialog to appear in a way that suggests that something has gone wrong. This includes an 'error' image, an appropriate title and header, and just a ButtonType.OK button for the user to click on to dismiss the dialog.

info.name: EXCEPTION
info.doc: The [EXCEPTION](tools.aqua.bgw.dialog/DialogType/EXCEPTION) dialog type configures the dialog to show an exception stack trace. This includes an 'exception' image, an appropriate title and header, and just a ButtonType.DISMISS button for the user to click on to dismiss the dialog.

info.name: INFORMATION
info.doc: The [INFORMATION](tools.aqua.bgw.dialog/DialogType/INFORMATION) dialog type configures the dialog to appear in a way that suggests the content of the dialog is informing the user of a piece of information. This includes an 'information' image, an appropriate title and header, and just a ButtonType.OK button for the user to click on to dismiss the dialog.

info.name: NONE
info.doc: The [NONE](tools.aqua.bgw.dialog/DialogType/NONE) dialog type has the effect of not setting any default properties in the dialog besides an ButtonType.OK button for the user to click on to dismiss the dialog.

info.name: WARNING
info.doc: The [WARNING](tools.aqua.bgw.dialog/DialogType/WARNING) dialog type configures the dialog to appear in a way that suggests the content of the dialog is warning the user about some fact or action. This includes a 'warning' image, an appropriate title and header, and just a ButtonType.OK button for the user to click on to dismiss the dialog.

info.name: FileDialog
info.signature: class FileDialog(val mode: FileDialogMode, val title: String = "", val initialFileName: String = "", val initialDirectoryPath: String? = null, val extensionFilters: List<ExtensionFilter> = emptyList())
info.doc: A [FileDialog](tools.aqua.bgw.dialog/FileDialog) such as a file chooser or save dialog, depending on [FileDialogMode](tools.aqua.bgw.dialog/FileDialogMode).

properties.name: extensionFilters
properties.doc: Extensions filters for this file chooser.

properties.name: initialFileName
properties.doc: The initial file name in the file name text box.

properties.name: mode
properties.doc: The Dialog's mode.

properties.name: onPathsSelected
properties.doc: Gets invoked whenever this [FileDialog](tools.aqua.bgw.dialog/FileDialog) is used to select file(s). It always returns a list of selected file paths.

properties.name: onSelectionCancelled
properties.doc: Gets invoked whenever this [FileDialog](tools.aqua.bgw.dialog/FileDialog) is closed without selecting a path or the selection was cancelled.

properties.name: title
properties.doc: The Dialog's title text.

info.name: onPathsSelected
info.doc:
Gets invoked whenever this [FileDialog](tools.aqua.bgw.dialog/FileDialog) is used to select file(s). It always returns a list of selected file paths.

If mode is [FileDialogMode.OPEN_FILE](tools.aqua.bgw.dialog/FileDialogMode/OPEN_FILE), [FileDialogMode.SAVE_FILE](tools.aqua.bgw.dialog/FileDialogMode/SAVE_FILE) or [FileDialogMode.CHOOSE_DIRECTORY](tools.aqua.bgw.dialog/FileDialogMode/CHOOSE_DIRECTORY) it will always contain exactly one element.

If mode is [FileDialogMode.OPEN_MULTIPLE_FILES](tools.aqua.bgw.dialog/FileDialogMode/OPEN_MULTIPLE_FILES) it will contain multiple elements.

info.name: onSelectionCancelled
info.doc: Gets invoked whenever this [FileDialog](tools.aqua.bgw.dialog/FileDialog) is closed without selecting a path or the selection was cancelled.

info.name: CHOOSE_DIRECTORY
info.doc: Mode so select a directory.

info.name: A
info.doc: Constant for the A key.

properties.name: string
properties.doc: Key name.

functions.name: isArrow
functions.doc: Returns true if key was an arrow key, false otherwise.

functions.name: isDigit
functions.doc: Returns true if key was digit key 1-9 from key- or numpad, false otherwise.

functions.name: isFunction
functions.doc: Returns true if key was a function key F1, F2, etc., false otherwise.

functions.name: isLetter
functions.doc: Returns true if key was a letter key A-Z, false otherwise.

functions.name: isModifier
functions.doc: Returns true if key was a modifier like Shift or Ctrl etc., false otherwise.

functions.name: isNavigation
functions.doc: Returns true if key was a navigation key like arrows and page up / down, false otherwise.

functions.name: isOnNumpad
functions.doc: Returns true if key was on numpad like numpad numbers or +,-,/,* etc., false otherwise.

functions.name: isWhitespace
functions.doc: Returns true if key was a whitespace key like space, tab etc., false otherwise.

functions.name: toString
functions.doc: Returns a string representation of the key.

functions.doc: Returns true if key was an arrow key, false otherwise.

functions.name: isDigit
functions.doc: Returns true if key was digit key 1-9 from key- or numpad, false otherwise.

functions.name: isFunction
functions.doc: Returns true if key was a function key F1, F2, etc., false otherwise.

functions.name: isLetter
functions.doc: Returns true if key was a letter key A-Z, false otherwise.

functions.name: isModifier
functions.doc: Returns true if key was a modifier like Shift or Ctrl etc., false otherwise.

functions.name: isNavigation
functions.doc: Returns true if key was a navigation key like arrows and page up / down, false otherwise.

functions.name: isOnNumpad
functions.doc: Returns true if key was on numpad like numpad numbers or +,-,/,* etc., false otherwise.

functions.name: isWhitespace
functions.doc: Returns true if key was a whitespace key like space, tab etc., false otherwise.

functions.name: toString
functions.doc: Returns a string representation of the key.

info.doc:
Called when an opponent sent a GameActionMessage. This method is supposed to act as a fallback solution if not for all Subclasses of GameAction a dedicated handler was registered using GameActionReceiver annotation.

Register a message receiver for a message type 'ExampleGameAction' by declaring a function

fun yourFunctionName(yourParameterName1 : ExampleGameAction, yourParameterName2: String) { }

The BGW framework will automatically choose a function to invoke based on the declared parameter types.

info.name: formatMessage
info.doc:
Formats the message. Override this method to provide a formatted message. If this method returns null, [toString](tools.aqua.bgw.net.common/GameAction/toString) will be used instead.

But don't rely on [toString](tools.aqua.bgw.net.common/GameAction/toString) to format the message in a readable format. Thus implement this method if you want to a properly formatted message to debug or display.

info.name: GameAction
info.doc:
Baseclass for all Game action classes.

Extend this class in order to create data classes that can be sent by a BoardGameClient. All implementations additionally need the [GameActionClass](tools.aqua.bgw.net.common.annotations/GameActionClass) annotation to work properly.

info.name: GameActionReceiver

Annotation for Game action receiver functions.

Use this annotation on any receiver function with [GameActionClass](tools.aqua.bgw.net.common.annotations/GameActionClass) as parameter. All annotated functions will be indexed upon runtime and mapped to GameActionClasses.

info.signature: class CreateGameResponse(val status: CreateGameResponseStatus, val sessionID: String?) : Response
info.doc: Response upon [tools.aqua.bgw.net.common.request.CreateGameMessage](tools.aqua.bgw.net.common.request/CreateGameMessage).

properties.doc: Session ID for this game. `null` if creation was not successful.

properties.doc: Status code.

info.name: ALREADY_ASSOCIATED_WITH_GAME
info.doc: This connection was already associated with a game on the server. No Game was created.

info.name: GAME_ID_DOES_NOT_EXIST
info.doc: There was no Game associated with the GameID. No Game was created.

info.name: SERVER_ERROR
info.doc: Something on the server went wrong. No Game was created.

info.name: SESSION_WITH_ID_ALREADY_EXISTS
info.doc: A Session with the specified ID already exists on the server. No Game was created.

info.name: SUCCESS
info.doc: Created the game successfully.

info.name: remove
info.doc:
Removes the first occurrence of the specified element from this list, if it is present.

If the list does not contain the element, it is unchanged.

More formally, removes the element with the lowest index i such that Objects.equals(o, get(i)) (if such an element exists).

Returns true if this list contained the specified element (or equivalently, if this list changed as a result of the call).

parameters.name: o
parameters.doc: Element to be removed from this list, if present.

info.name: retainAll
info.doc:
Retains only the elements in this list that are contained in the specified collection.

In other words, removes from this list all of its elements that are not contained in the specified collection.

parameters.name: elements
parameters.doc: Collection containing elements to be retained in this list.

throws.doc: If the class of an element of this list is incompatible with the specified collection.

throws.name: NullPointerException
throws.doc: If this list contains a null element and the specified collection does not permit null elements.

info.name: subList
info.signature: fun subList(fromIndex: Int, toIndex: Int): List<T>
info.doc:
Returns a view of the portion of this list between the specified fromIndex inclusive and toIndex exclusive. (If fromIndex and toIndex are equal, the returned list is empty.)

The returned list is backed by this list, so non-structural changes in the returned list are reflected in this list, and vice-versa.

The returned list supports all optional list operations.

This method eliminates the need for explicit range operations (of the sort that commonly exist for arrays).

Any operation that expects a list can be used as a range operation by passing a subList view instead of a whole list.

For example, the following idiom removes a range of elements from a list:

throws.name: IndexOutOfBoundsException
throws.doc: If any index exceeds the list's bounds.

throws.name: IllegalArgumentException
throws.doc: If the endpoint indices are out of order (fromIndex > toIndex).

info.name: contains
info.doc:
Returns true if this list contains the specified element.

More formally, returns true if and only if this list contains at least one element e such that Objects.equals(o, e).

parameters.name: o
parameters.doc: Element whose presence in this list is to be tested.

info.name: indexOf
info.doc:
Returns the index of the first occurrence of the specified element in this list, or -1 if this list does not contain the element.

More formally, returns the lowest index i such that Objects.equals(o, get(i)), or -1 if there is no such index.

info.name: LimitedDoubleProperty
info.signature: open class LimitedDoubleProperty(lowerBoundInclusive: Number = Double.NEGATIVE_INFINITY, upperBoundInclusive: Number = Double.POSITIVE_INFINITY, initialValue: Number = lowerBoundInclusive) : Property<Double>
info.doc:
A limited DoubleProperty to a value range. Value will be checked to be in range lowerBoundInclusive to upperBoundInclusive.

Therefore upperBoundInclusive must be greater or equal to lowerBoundInclusive. The Range is constant and cannot be altered after object creation.

throws.name: IllegalArgumentException
throws.doc: If a value out of range is set as initialValue.

constructors.signature: constructor(lowerBoundInclusive: Number = Double.NEGATIVE_INFINITY, upperBoundInclusive: Number = Double.POSITIVE_INFINITY, initialValue: Number = lowerBoundInclusive)

info.name: BidirectionalMap
info.doc: Creates a map with the given set of elements mapping pair.first ->pair.second.

parameters.name: elements
parameters.doc: Elements to be initialized in the map.

info.name: addAll
info.signature: fun addAll(vararg items: Pair<T, R>): Boolean
info.doc:
Adds all relations A -> B. If any of the given items already exist, it gets ignored. If any item contains a key or value that already exists, the map remains unchanged.

Using the example map of (A -> B), (C -> D):

addAll(E -> F), (G -> H) results in (A -> B), (C -> D), (E -> F), (G -> H) : true.

addAll(A -> B), (E -> F) results in (A -> B), (C -> D), (E -> F) : true.

addAll(A -> C), (E -> F) results in (A -> B), (C -> D) : false.

info.name: addAll
info.doc:
Adds all relations A -> B. If any of the given elements already exist, it gets ignored. If any element contains a key or value that already exists, the map remains unchanged.

Using the example map of (A -> B), (C -> D):

addAll(E -> F), (G -> H) results in (A -> B), (C -> D), (E -> F), (G -> H) : true.

addAll(A -> B), (E -> F) results in (A -> B), (C -> D), (E -> F) : true.

addAll(A -> C), (E -> F) results in (A -> B), (C -> D) : false.

info.name: BidirectionalMap
info.signature: open class BidirectionalMap<T : Any, R : Any>(elements: Pair<T, R>)
info.doc:
A bidirectional map. Each key-value-pair gets mapped in both directions. Keys and values must be unique in the sense that there must not be a duplicate key in the domain, nor a duplicate value in the coDomain, but the same element may appear once as key and once as value.

Example:

(A -> B), (A -> C)

is invalid because A is contained twice in the Domain.

(A -> B), (C -> B)

is invalid because B is contained twice in the coDomain.

(A -> B), (C -> A)

is valid because A is only contained once in the domain and in the coDomain.

parameters.name: elements
parameters.doc: Elements to be initialized in the map.

properties.doc: Represents the entries of this map as a set.

properties.doc: Represents the co-domain keys of this map as a set.

properties.name: keysForward
properties.doc: Represents the domain keys of this map as a set.

types.name: BidirectionalMap
types.doc: A bidirectional map. Each key-value-pair gets mapped in both directions. Keys and values must be unique in the sense that there must not be a duplicate key in the domain, nor a duplicate value in the coDomain, but the same element may appear once as key and once as value.

info.name: ColorVisual
info.signature: constructor(r: Int, g: Int, b: Int, alpha: Number = 1.0)
info.doc:
Creates a solid [ColorVisual](tools.aqua.bgw.visual/ColorVisual) filled with given RGBA values.

The alpha channel gets multiplied with the [transparency](SingleLayerVisual/transparency) i.e. alpha = 0.5 (50%) and [transparency](SingleLayerVisual/transparency) = 0.5 (50%) leads to 25% visibility / 75% [transparency](SingleLayerVisual/transparency).

parameters.name: alpha
parameters.doc: Alpha channel (0 - 255 for Int, 0.0 - 1.0 for Double). Value is 1.0 by default and if parameter is out of a valid range.

info.name: color
info.doc:
The displayed [Color](tools.aqua.bgw.core/Color) of this [Visual](tools.aqua.bgw.visual/Visual).

The alpha channel gets multiplied with the [transparency](tools.aqua.bgw.visual/SingleLayerVisual/transparency) i.e. alpha = 128 (50%) and [transparency](tools.aqua.bgw.visual/SingleLayerVisual/transparency) = 0.5 (50%) leads to 25% visibility / 75% [transparency](tools.aqua.bgw.visual/SingleLayerVisual/transparency).

info.name: copy
info.doc: Copies this [ColorVisual](tools.aqua.bgw.visual/ColorVisual) to a new object.

info.name: CompoundVisual
info.doc:
A compound visual containing stacked SingleLayerVisuals.

Hint: Each [SingleLayerVisual](tools.aqua.bgw.visual/SingleLayerVisual) besides the bottom should have opacity in order to display all layers properly.

parameters.name: children
parameters.doc: Children SingleLayerVisuals in the order they should be displayed, where the first [SingleLayerVisual](tools.aqua.bgw.visual/SingleLayerVisual) gets displayed at the bottom of the stack.

info.name: ImageVisual
info.signature: open class ImageVisual(path: String, val width: Int = -1, val height: Int = -1, val offsetX: Int = 0, val offsetY: Int = 0, rotation: Number = 0) : SingleLayerVisual
info.doc:
Visual showing an Image.

The image gets stretched to the size of the component that this visual is embedded in.

A sub-image can be loaded by passing offsetX/offsetY for the TOP_LEFT corner and width/height for the sub-image size. If width or height is passed as -1, the remaining image from offsetX/offsetY will be loaded.

parameters.name: path
parameters.doc: Location of image file relative to "resources".

parameters.name: rotation
parameters.doc: Rotation of the image in degrees. Default: 0.

throws.name: IllegalArgumentException
throws.doc: If path is not a valid path or empty or if path was not found in resources.

properties.name: height
properties.doc: Height of sub-image. Pass -1 to use full height. Default: -1.

properties.name: offsetX
properties.doc: Left bound of sub-image. Default: 0.

properties.name: offsetY
properties.doc: Top bound of sub-image. Default: 0.

properties.name: width
